turns.name: CHIEF JUSTICE ROBERTS
turns.text: Justice Gorsuch has the opinion of the Court this morning in Case 22-1178, FBI versus Fikre.

turns.name: JUSTICE GORSUCH
turns.text: This case involves the No Fly List that prohibits certain individuals from traveling on aircraft in the United States. Many of the details surrounding the No Fly List are not publicly available. It appears, for example, that no statute or publicly issued regulations describes the standards the government employs when adding individuals to or removing them from the list. Yonas Fikre, a U.S. citizen, brought this suit alleging that the government placed him on the list in violation of his federal constitutional rights. More specifically, he alleged that the government targeted him because of his religious faith and that it did so without providing him notice, an opportunity to be heard, or any other incident of procedural due process. After he brought his suit, the government removed Mr. Fikre from the No Fly List and sought to have his claims dismissed as moot before trial. The Ninth Circuit rejected the government’s motion to dismiss the case, holding that a defendant’s voluntary cessation of challenged conduct does not automatically moot a case, nor the Court held, was a declaration the government offered in connection with its motion enough to moot the case. The government asked us to review the Ninth Circuit’s decision, and we agreed to do so. For reasons explained in our decision today, we agree with the Ninth Circuit that this case is not moot. In doing so, we do not pass on the merits of Mr. Fikre’s claims. We hold only that a Federal Court presently has the power to resolve them. The decision of the Court is unanimous. Justice Alito has filed a concurring opinion in which Justice Kavanaugh has joined.
